phrases.cultural_note: CERN offers free guided tours that must be booked well in advance online. The Science Gateway visitor centre is open to the public without reservation.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the visitor entrance is
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve l'entrée pour les visiteurs ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lon-TRAY poor lay vee-zee-TUR
phrases.cultural_note: The main public entrance for visitors is at Reception Building 33, near the Globe of Science and Innovation on Route de Meyrin.

responses.english: Across the way, through the main gate.
responses.phonetic: ahn FAHSS, par luh por-TYE prehn-see-PAL
responses.local: En face, par le portail principal.

phrases.intent_en: Ask how long the CERN visit takes
phrases.text_local: La visite dure combien de temps ?
phrases.pronunciation: lah vee-ZEET DOOR kohm-BYEHN duh TAHN
phrases.cultural_note: CERN visits typically last between one and three hours depending on the tour type.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about public transport to CERN
phrases.text_local: Comment venir au CERN en transports en commun ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MOHN vuh-NEER oh SAIRN on trohnss-POR on koh-MUN
phrases.cultural_note: Tram line 18 runs from Geneva city centre directly to the CERN stop, making it an easy 30-minute journey.

phrases.text_local: Qu'est-ce que le Science Gateway propose au public ?
phrases.pronunciation: kes-KUH luh SY-onss GAYT-way proh-POHZ oh poo-BLEEK
phrases.cultural_note: Opened in 2023, the Science Gateway is CERN's free public facility with interactive exhibitions about particle physics for all ages.

responses.english: It's right at the entrance, free.

phrases.text_local: C'est incroyable de visiter l'endroit où le World Wide Web a été inventé !
phrases.pronunciation: seh tan-krwa-YAH-bluh duh vee-zee-TAY lon-DRWA oo luh WURLD WYD WEB ah ay-TAY an-von-TAY
phrases.cultural_note: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN in 1989 — a fact that surprises many visitors who associate CERN only with particle physics.
